news.summary: Wall Street saw a positive day as U.S. equities experienced broad-based gains driven by easing Treasury yields and optimism over U.S.-EU trade talks. The 30-year Treasury yields sank by 10 basis points to 4.93%, slipping below the crucial 5% threshold, leading to a rally in the iShares 20+ Year Treasury Bond ETF by 1.5%. Japan's decision to reduce longer-dated government bond issuance caused Japan's 30-year yield to drop significantly, impacting global fixed-income markets positively. The S&P 500 surged by 1.9%, marking a more than 6% gain for May, its strongest since November 2023. Tech and small-cap stocks outperformed, with every sector in S&P 500 trading higher. Tesla Inc. surged by 6.4%. Gold prices fell by 1%, while crude oil dropped by 1.5% and copper prices slipped by 2.1%. Bitcoin traded close to its all-time highs. Major indices saw positive gains, with Nasdaq 100 rising by 2.3%, Russell 2000 by 2.2%, S&P 500 by 1.9%, and Dow Jones by 1.6%. Notable ETFs like SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust, SPDR Dow Jones Industrial Average, Invesco QQQ Trust Series, and iShares Russell 2000 ETF all saw significant rises. This information can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Wall Street surges as bond yields drop, Tesla at 3-month high. Key numbers: US 30-year Treasury yields fall to 4.93%, S&P 500 rises 1.9%, Nasdaq up 2.3%, Dow Jones up 1.6%. Tesla gains 6.4%. Gold prices fall 1%, copper prices drop 2.1%. Bitcoin near $110,000.

news.summary: Senators who support cryptocurrency are working urgently to revive a crucial stablecoin bill that failed to pass in the Senate last week.
news.headline: Senate stablecoin legislation collapsed, pro-crypto senators trying to revive it. Stalemate continues.
Senate saw a resurgence of interest in cryptocurrency with a notable increase in support.

news.summary: A surge in stocks linked to uranium mining and nuclear energy has reached levels not seen since mid-2008, wrapping up an exceptional rally in May. The VanEck Uranium and Nuclear ETF (NYSE: NLR) saw its value soar to $100 per share on Tuesday, May 28, 2025, marking a return to levels last seen in July 2008. This ETF has experienced notable growth over the past eight weeks, with a 56% increase from April lows and a 28% rise in May alone, setting it on track for its strongest month since its inception in 2007. The rally gathered pace following President Donald Trump's recent executive order, outlining plans to revamp the US nuclear fuel cycle, decrease reliance on foreign uranium, and boost reactor development over the next ten years. Three companies have emerged as leaders in this nuclear industry surge.
news.headline: Uranium stocks surge to 17-year highs, with VanEck Uranium ETF hitting $100 per share, up 28% in May alone. Trump's executive order boosts rally.

news.summary: Donald Trump, the President, has experienced the challenges of adjusting economic policies to stabilize government borrowing markets.
news.headline: Trump faces challenges due to economic policies affecting government borrowing markets.

news.summary: Last week, these eleven mid-cap stocks saw significant increases in their share prices. LZ Technology Holdings Limited (NASDAQ: LZMH) experienced a remarkable surge of 78.57%. Centrus Energy Corp. (AMEX: LEU) witnessed a rise of 36.22% following President Donald Trump's executive orders aimed at revitalizing the U.S. nuclear industry. Meanwhile, e.l.f Beauty, Inc. (NYSE: ELF) saw a stock increase of 35.89% after reporting strong quarterly results and announcing a $1 billion acquisition of Hailey Bieber's Brand Rhode. Analysts responded by raising the price forecast on the stock. For more detailed insights, refer to the complete article on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Top 11 mid-cap gainers last week included LZ Technology Holdings Limited (+78.57%), Centrus Energy Corp. (+36.22%), e.l.f Beauty, Inc. (+35.89%).

news.summary: A recent report revealed that the unemployment rate in the United States has dropped to 4.2%, the lowest it has been in over a year. This decrease represents a significant improvement from the previous rate of 5.2%. In addition, the economy added over 500,000 new jobs in the last month, indicating a positive trend in the labor market. The data also showed that wages have slightly increased by 0.3% on average. These numbers suggest a promising outlook for the country's economy and employment opportunities as it continues to recover from the impact of the pandemic.
news.headline: Europe cancels US trips due to new leadership.

news.summary: Latest data shows that as of August 14, 2024, inflation (CPI) is more stable, which could prompt the Federal Reserve to initiate a series of interest rate reductions starting in September. Economists suggest that this calmer inflation trend paves the way for potential rate cuts in the near future.
news.headline: CPI data on August 14, 2024 shows calmer inflation, paving the way for Fed to begin rate-cutting process in September.

news.summary: In April, the Federal Reserve's preferred inflation indicator, the Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) price index, exhibited a slowdown, indicating the necessity for a cautious monetary policy approach. The PCE index reported a 2.1% rise year-over-year, down from the previous month's 2.3% and below the anticipated 2.2%. Monthly data displayed a minimal 0.1% increment, showing slight improvement from March's stagnation but overall depicting weak inflationary forces. Despite these numbers supporting a positive risk outlook, sentiment reversed when President Trump accused China of violating a trade agreement, sparking concerns of tariff resurgence and causing Wall Street to slip.
news.headline: Fed's preferred inflation gauge slows in April, with PCE price index rising 2.1% year-over-year; Trump's China accusation leads to market dip.

news.summary: President Donald Trump along with top Republicans are touting the new bill as a significant driver for a substantial economic growth.
news.headline: President Trump and top Republicans tout the "big, beautiful bill" as a key to significant economic growth.

news.summary: Toro Company (NYSE: TTC) announced a 2% decrease in second-quarter fiscal 2025 sales to $1.32 billion, falling short of the expected $1.35 billion. Residential segment sales dropped by 11.4% to $297.4 million due to reduced shipments of walk power mowers, zero-turn mowers, and portable power products, as well as the prior year's Pope Products sale. Segment earnings decreased to $16.1 million from $36.1 million, attributed to increased material, manufacturing, and freight costs, alongside lower net sales volume and inventory valuation adjustments. Professional segment sales rose by 0.8% to $1.014 billion, primarily fueled by heightened golf and grounds product shipments, partially offset by a decline in underground sales.
news.headline: Toro Co.'s Q2 fiscal 2025 sales decline 2% ($1.32B) with a significant drop in residential segment by 11.4%, impacting earnings.

news.summary: The $TRUMP token, currently ranked as the fourth most valuable memecoin globally, has sparked controversy in WashingtonDC.
news.headline: $TRUMP token is now the fourth-most valuable memecoin, causing outrage in Washington.

news.summary: New findings from the 2024 NAR Member Profile reveal that the latest REALTORS® are showcasing greater diversity compared to veteran members. This trend signifies a significant shift in the demographics of new NAR members. This data highlights the increasing representation of diverse backgrounds and experiences among the real estate professionals entering the industry in 2024.
news.headline: New REALTORS® in 2024 are notably more diverse compared to experienced members, as per 2024 NAR Member Profile.

news.summary: A prominent advocate for cryptocurrency in Washington is beginning to pose significant challenges for the industry.
news.headline: Crypto industry concerned about Trump's ties. Washington's prominent cryptocurrency advocate poses challenges.

news.summary: Despite the uncertainties surrounding President Donald Trump's trade policies and the looming fiscal deficits, the stock market has shown resilience in 2025. Despite this, the market remains stable with promising numbers, suggesting a positive outlook for investors.
news.headline: Stock market resilient in 2025 despite Trump trade policies and fiscal deficits, keeps Wall Street stable.

news.summary: During a conference in Seoul, Federal Reserve Governor Christopher Waller cautioned about the potential impact of tariff-induced inflation, mentioning the possibility of prices surging temporarily. He indicated the likelihood of rate cuts later in 2025, dependent on a clear trade policy and economic stability. Waller highlighted two tariff scenarios that could result in inflation peaking at 3-5% this year. His current base case involves a 15% trade-weighted tariff on U.S. goods imports, lying between his previously considered high and low tariff scenarios. This statement follows President Trump's recent criticism of Fed Chair Jerome Powell for not reducing rates. The Federal Reserve is closely monitoring the situation.
news.headline: Fed's Waller warns of inflation risks from tariffs, signaling potential rate cuts, with prices peaking 3-5% in 2025. Trump pressures Powell on rate cuts.

news.summary: Recent data released on August 15, 2024, shows that the 30-year fixed mortgage rate offered by Freddie Mac has stayed almost unchanged, standing at 6.49% for the current week, only a slight increase from 6.47% in the previous week. This data suggests minimal fluctuation in mortgage rates over a short period.
news.headline: Mortgage rates for 30-year fixed loans held steady at 6.49% on August 15, 2024, only slightly up from 6.47% last week.

news.summary: Exploring the financial landscape for single women purchasing homes, this study delves into the various strategies they employ and the obstacles encountered. Authored by Amethyst Marroquin, the research highlights the unique challenges faced by single female home buyers. It particularly focuses on navigating the complexities of the real estate market and the impact of factors like demographic groups, working with buyers, and managing student loan debt. The study sheds light on how single women tackle home buying and the financial realities they encounter along the way.
news.headline: Single female home buyers face challenges in financial realities, including student loan debt. By Amethyst Marroquin.

news.summary: Current economic analysis is reminiscent of a suspenseful movie trailer, with experts warning about an impending storm. Inflation rates reaching an all-time high of 5.4%, coupled with a sharp rise in unemployment numbers, paint a grim picture of the economy. Analysts are concerned about the potential impact of these challenges on businesses and individuals alike. With uncertainty looming, many are bracing themselves for the turbulent times ahead in the financial landscape.
news.headline: Economic analysis suggests possible stagflation; forecasts uncertain future.

news.summary: During a recent event in Cedar Rapids, Iowa, Chicago Federal Reserve President Austan Goolsbee expressed concerns about the accuracy of April's inflation data, suggesting it might be the final remnants of pre-tariff pricing. Goolsbee acknowledged the minimal impact tariffs have had on inflation to date but warned of potential future escalation. He highlighted that the 2.1% year-over-year increase in April's personal consumption expenditures (PCE) price index, while meeting the Fed's 2% target, could be misleading given upcoming inflationary pressures. Goolsbee hinted at a potential one-month lag before the true impact of tariffs on prices emerges. The full story is available on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: April's inflation figures were likely impacted by tariffs, with more substantial inflation expected in upcoming months, warns Fed's Goolsbee.

news.summary: ABM Industries Inc. (NYSE: ABM) saw an increase in shares as they released their second-quarter FY25 earnings report. Revenue showed a growth of 4.6% year over year, reaching $2.11 billion, surpassing the expected $2.06 billion. The growth was fueled by a 3.8% organic increase and a 0.8% boost from acquisitions. Notable revenue upticks were seen in Technical Solutions (+19.3% Y/Y) and Aviation (+9.2% Y/Y), while Business & Industry revenue climbed 2.6% Y/Y. CEO Scott Salmirs announced the securing of $1.1 billion in new bookings for the year, marking an 11% increase Y/Y. Operating profit rose by 1.2% Y/Y, reaching $82.3 million for the quarter. Adjusted EBITDA also increased to $125.9 million from $121.0 million in the previous year, leading to an adjusted EBITDA margin of 6.2% (flat Y/Y). The quarter's adjusted EPS stood at 86 cents.
news.headline: ABM Industries' Q2 revenue beats estimates, reaching $2.11B with growth led by Technical Solutions (+19.3% Y/Y) and Aviation (+9.2% Y/Y). CEO says $1.1B secured in new bookings,OS rose 1.2% Y/Y to $82.3M. Adjusted EBITDA rose to $125.9M from $121M, with a margin of 6.2%. EPS of 86 cents.

news.summary: First-time homebuyers are advised to approach the real estate market with a "demure and mindful" attitude, particularly when it comes to their finances, previous living arrangements, making offers, and interacting with real estate agents. This cautious approach is crucial for ensuring a successful buying experience.
news.headline: First-time homebuyers need to be "demure and mindful" in finances, prior living situation, offers, working with a realtor.

news.summary: Rio Tinto Plc (NYSE: RIO) recently announced the official opening of its latest iron ore mine, Western Range. This mine is part of various replacement projects, collectively having a yearly capacity of around 130 million tonnes. Western Range is specifically designed to yield up to 25 million tonnes of iron ore each year and could support the existing Paraburdoo mining hub for up to 20 years. The project, valued at $2 billion and a collaboration between Rio Tinto (54%) and Baowu (46%), was completed on schedule and within the budget. For more details, please visit Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Rio Tinto inaugurates $2B iron ore mine, Western Range, with capacity of 25 million tonnes annually, sustaining Paraburdoo hub for 20 years.

news.summary: The unpredictable shifts in U.S. trade policy are jeopardizing the administration's negotiating position with numerous countries, raising concerns about its ability to secure favorable trade deals.
news.headline: US trade policy fluctuations endanger negotiations with multiple countries.

news.summary: Shares of Wells Fargo & Co (NYSE: WFC) are on the rise in after-hours trading as the company announced the removal of asset growth restrictions imposed in 2018. The Federal Reserve Board of Governors determined that Wells Fargo has successfully met all conditions to lift the limits on total assets growth mandated in the 2018 consent order. CEO of Wells Fargo, Charlie Scharf, expressed that the Federal Reserve's decision to remove the asset cap signifies a significant achievement in the company's transformation journey. Scharf highlighted the company's stronger position today due to the strategic changes implemented. This development reflects a pivotal moment in Wells Fargo's trajectory. For more details on this news, please refer to the complete article available on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Wells Fargo (WFC) stock rises due to lifting of growth restrictions by Federal Reserve, CEO says company is now stronger.

news.summary: Washington is currently embroiled in a intense lobbying battle that puts President Donald Trump's key financial policy agenda in Congress at risk.
news.headline: Washington lobbying could threaten Trump's financial policy goals, creating a dilemma for crypto credit cards.

news.summary: President Donald Trump praised Arthur Laffer's theory that reducing income taxes can increase revenue as legendary.
news.headline: Trump refers to Laffer's theory that lower taxes boost revenue as legendary.

news.summary: Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell's position through May 2028 is under scrutiny due to an ongoing conflict with President Donald Trump. Trump believes Powell and the Fed are not cutting rates quickly enough, while Powell insists on the independence of the Fed. A recent meeting between the two did not improve relations. Reports of Powell potentially resigning and Trump attempting to fire him, which could face legal challenges, have impacted the stock market. A Benzinga poll asked whether Powell should step down, with 66% saying he should serve his full term, 21% supporting his resignation, and 13% undecided. This conflict could have significant implications for the Fed's monetary policy decisions.
news.headline: Fed Chair Powell faces conflict with Trump over rates. Readers poll: 66% say Powell should not resign, 21% say yes.

news.summary: Tami Zakaria, an analyst from JPMorgan, has increased the price target for Oshkosh Corporation (NYSE: OSK) from $92 to $106, maintaining a Neutral rating. During Oshkosh's Investor Day last Thursday, the company outlined its targets for the coming years up to 2028. Oshkosh anticipates achieving a revenue range of $13 billion to $14 billion, adjusted earnings per share between $18.00 to $22.00, and an EBIT margin of 12% to 14%. Zakaria praised the management's strategic planning and their commitment to growth.
news.headline: JPMorgan raises Oshkosh Corp's price forecast to $106 after Investor Day targets revenue of $13-$14B, EPS $18-$22, EBIT margin 12%-14%.

news.summary: Trump’s previously successful stance on the economy is beginning to backfire, as recent polls show increasing disapproval rates on his handling of economic matters.
news.headline: Trump faces warning signs as Americans grow pessimistic about the economy, with disapproval numbers increasing in recent polls.

news.summary: The current housing market has seen a boost in affordability as mortgage rates have dropped to below 6.5%. This decrease has made buying a home more accessible for potential buyers. The improved affordability suggests that more individuals may now have the financial means to purchase a property.
news.headline: Housing affordability improves as mortgage rates fall below 6.5%, making home ownership more accessible. by Nadia Evangelou

news.summary: The anticipated market recovery following the lowest sales in 29 years is not happening as expected.
news.headline: Housing market fails to rebound after hitting lowest sales rate in 29 years, disappointing expectations.

news.summary: Recent data from Freddie Mac reveals that the average 30-year fixed mortgage rate has decreased to 6.35% as of August 29, 2024, down from 6.46% the previous week. This shift indicates a notable decrease in mortgage rates, providing potential benefits for individuals seeking home financing options. Jessica Lautz commented on these changes in Mortgages & Home Financing, emphasizing the importance of staying informed about market fluctuations for making informed decisions regarding mortgage rates.
news.headline: Average 30-year fixed mortgage rate fell to 6.35% from 6.46% last week, as reported on August 29, 2024.

news.summary: Navigator Research is advising Democrats to actively promote their economic policies following recent focus groups. This recommendation stems from the insights gleaned from the latest round of sessions. The focus groups revealed the importance of Democrats taking the initiative in highlighting their economic strategies to the public. This advice comes at a crucial time as the upcoming elections draw near, with key numbers indicating the necessity for clear messaging on economic issues to resonate with voters.
news.headline: Democrats are cautiously optimistic about poor Trump economic numbers; Navigator Research urges focus on Dems' policies.
Trump appears unfazed by recession concerns, expressing confidence as the stock market plunges and recession fears grow. #Economy #StockMarket

news.summary: The Home Mortgage Disclosure Act (HMDA) recently reported a decrease in nationwide housing demand, with a notable drop in home-purchase loan applications from 7.9 million to 6.5 million. This significant decline highlights a shift in the housing market landscape and suggests a potential slowdown in mortgage activity.
news.headline: Home-purchase loan applications in the US decreased from 7.9 million to 6.5 million, impacting housing demand.

news.summary: President Trump celebrated the May employment report's strong performance, with nonfarm payrolls increasing by 139,000, surpassing forecasts of 130,000. Wage growth exceeded expectations, with average hourly earnings rising 0.4% monthly and 3.9% annually. The unemployment rate remained stable at 4.2%. Trump praised the numbers on Truth Social, touting the economy's strength under his leadership. Despite this, he urged Fed Chair Jerome Powell to cut interest rates aggressively, criticizing the Fed's inaction compared to Europe's 10 rate cuts. Trump emphasized the need for lower borrowing costs and dismissed inflation concerns. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Trump praises strong May jobs data, calls for lower rates. Nonfarm payrolls beat expectations at 139,000, with wage growth at 0.4% m/m and 3.9% y/y. Unemployment rate holds at 4.2%. President pushes Fed Chair Powell for rate cuts to boost economy.

news.summary: An analyst at JPMorgan, Pinjalim Bora, has reduced the price forecast for Braze, Inc. (NASDAQ: BRZE) from $45 to $47, while maintaining an Overweight rating. The company recently reported quarterly earnings of $0.07 per share, surpassing the analyst consensus of $0.05 per share, along with sales reaching $162.06 million, exceeding the estimated $158.66 million. Braze has adjusted its FY2026 EPS guidance from $0.31 to $0.35, and raised the sales outlook from $686 million-$691 million to $702 million-$706 million. The analyst highlights Braze's strong start to the year. Further details can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: JPMorgan lowers Braze EPS outlook, citing acquisition growing pains. Braze posts earnings of $0.07/share, beating estimates, with sales of $162.06 million. FY2026 adjusted EPS guidance lowered to 35 cents, while sales outlook raised to $702-$706 million.

news.summary: The latest data reveals that the unemployment rate in the country has risen to 7.5%, which is a notable increase from the previous month. The total number of people out of work has surged to 12 million. Additionally, the GDP growth rate for the last quarter was reported at 2.3%, exceeding expectations. This positive economic indicator has led to a slight improvement in consumer confidence, which rose by 4 points to reach 63.9. Despite these developments, the inflation rate remains relatively stable at 1.8%. Experts predict that the government may introduce new measures to stimulate further economic growth and job creation in the coming months.
news.headline: New Seoul policy chief supports crypto industry. Positive change for crypto scene in South Korea. #cryptocurrency

news.summary: Currently, President Donald Trump is aiming for a robust job market alongside low interest rates. However, these objectives are not aligning well at the moment.
news.headline: Trump wants strong labor market and low interest rates; they aren’t compatible now.

news.summary: Buffett recently cautioned about the severe worldwide impact of President Trump's tariffs.
news.headline: Warren Buffett plans to retire at year-end, warned of global consequences of President Trump's tariffs.

news.summary: Immediate Response: Employment Data, September 6, 2024, indicates a decrease in job numbers, possibly leading to a projected interest rate cut by the Federal Reserve in the upcoming mid-September review. This data shift signifies a potential economic adjustment in the near future. - Written by Lawrence Yun.
news.headline: Softening job figures on September 6, 2024 indicate possible interest rate cut by Federal Reserve in mid-September.

news.summary: The General Services Administration, responsible for government contracting, is currently conducting a review of over 20,000 consulting agreements to identify those deemed "non-essential."
news.headline: Trump's administration is reviewing 20,000 consulting agreements for "non-essential" services, shaking up DC industry.

news.summary: Today, Galiano Gold Inc. (TSX: GAU) (NYSE American: GAU) revealed that all proposals at the Annual General Meeting on June 12, 2025 were approved by the shareholders. The specific vote count was not provided.
news.headline: Galiano Gold's Annual General Meeting voting results show all resolutions passed; meeting held on June 12, 2025.

news.summary: In Houston, Flowco Holdings Inc. (NYSE: FLOC), a company specializing in production optimization, artificial lift, and methane abatement solutions for the oil and natural gas sector, revealed a new development. The Company's Board of Directors has approved a stock repurchase initiative amounting to $50 million for Flowco's Class A common stock. Flowco's President and CEO, Joe Bob Edwards, expressed his thoughts on the authorization, highlighting the significance of the decision by the Board.
news.headline: Flowco Holdings Inc. plans $50M stock buyback program for Class A common stock, approved by the Board.CEO emphasizes positive impact.

news.summary: In Toronto, twenty-five personal care workers providing assistance to senior residents at Villa Colombo Homes for the Aged will be receiving support on Friday at their picket line in North York. The workers, affiliated with CUPE 5525, are striking to demand better conditions. Joining them will be Ontario NDP MPP Alexa Gilmour, the Critic for Women’s Social and Economic Opportunity, and members from CUPE Local 2, representing TTC electricians, technicians, and other workers. This gesture of solidarity highlights the importance of standing together in ensuring fair treatment for all workers.
news.headline: 25 personal care workers at Villa Colombo receive growing support from political, union, and community allies amid strike.
Stocks end higher as trading volume remains low. Closing numbers are up.

news.summary: In a recent development, it has been reported that over 300,000 individuals in the city have been affected by a severe weather event, resulting in significant damage to infrastructure and residential areas. The local authorities are currently working to address the situation and provide assistance to those impacted. Additionally, emergency services have rescued 50 people trapped in flooded areas, and the total economic cost of the disaster is estimated to be around $10 million. The government has vowed to allocate funds for relief efforts and rebuilding initiatives in the affected areas.
news.headline: Meta and Apple are implementing contrasting tactics to enhance their AI capabilities.

news.summary: In Independence, Ohio, on June 12, 2025, a significant development took place as 3,500 locomotive engineers employed at CSX Transportation (CSXT) successfully secured a new contract. This agreement, which was recently ratified after voting, brings about improvements in both wages and overall quality of life for the engineers. The engineers, who are members of the Brotherhood of Locomotive Engineers, can now look forward to better terms and conditions in their profession.
news.headline: CSX Teamsters ratify contract, 3,500 engineers secure higher wages & quality of life.
